input.content:
# Research: Foreground galaxy & galaxy-cluster distributions along the co-detected sightlines, and the methods used to search for them

**Date:** 2026-06-23
**Scope:** both internal codebase and external prior art
**Codebase state researched against:** commit `8ca87f7`, 2026-06-23 (branch `feature/radiometer-flux-cal`)
**Related Documents:**
- `docs/codetection-science-plan.md` (the parent science plan; §B/§D ground this question)
- `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-sightline-budget-sensitivity.md`
- `CONTEXT.md`, `docs/adr/0001-two-band-leverage-positioning.md`

## Question / Scope

For each of the 12 CHIME/DSA co-detected FRB sightlines, what foreground galaxies
and galaxy clusters lie along the line of sight, and what methods does the project
(and the wider literature) use to search for them? In scope: the production
foreground-search machinery, the search→DM/scattering-budget chain it feeds, the
actual per-sightline distributions currently committed, and the prior-art methods
for (a) intervening-galaxy identification, (b) cluster-intersection searches, and
(c) turning a foreground system into a DM/τ contribution. Out of scope: the
scattering-fit pipeline itself, host-galaxy (z ≥ z_FRB) association, and the
flux-calibration work on the current branch.

A defining property of the sample shapes everything below: **all 12 sightlines sit
at declination +70° to +74°** (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:21-34`), a sky region where
the standard optical survey footprints (SDSS, and the catalogs built on it) thin
out or vanish.

## Codebase Findings

### The sample
12 sightlines, Dec +70° to +74°, z from 0.043 to 0.51, with **three placeholder
z = 1.0** values (Freya, Mahi, Johndoeii) that flag an unmeasured host redshift
rather than a real one (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:21-34`,
`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:73-81`). Where z is a placeholder the cosmic and
host DM terms are withheld rather than fabricated
(`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:504-515`).

### Production foreground search (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py`)
- **Engines / catalogs.** NED cone search (`galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:23-54`) plus
  Vizier for GLADE+ (`VII/291`), DESI DR8-North (`VII/292`), and SDSS DR12
  (`V/147`) (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:37-41`, `galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:57-102`).
  An opt-in DESI DR1 spec-z TAP engine covers only 3/12 targets
  (Whitney/Phineas/Casey) and is **off by default**
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:218-221`, `galaxies/v2_0/config.py:45,55`). Enricher
  catalogs (Legacy DR10 tractor, AllWISE, GALEX, 2MASS-XSC) are likewise off by
  default (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:48-56`, `galaxies/v2_0/enrichers.py`).
- **Foreground criterion.** A row is foreground if `z < z_FRB + 0.01`, with a
  photo-z-error-aware relaxation (`z − 2σ_z < z_limit`)
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:56-74`, `config.py:13`).
- **Impact-parameter cut.** 100 kpc for galaxies, **5 Mpc for clusters**
  (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:9-11`, `search.py:71-74`). The query cone is sized to
  the larger limit and capped at 2° so low-z cluster cones don't time out Vizier
  (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:15-17`, `search.py:228-234`).
- **Dedup & photometry backfill.** Cross-catalog duplicates merged by 10″ + scaled
  Δz, keeping the smaller-photo-z-error / higher-rank source
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:87-149`); PS1 g/i magnitudes backfilled for rows
  lacking a catalog stellar mass — the only mass route for DESI-photo-z-only
  galaxies (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:152-201`, `engines.py:109-148`).

### Cluster handling — the main gap
Galaxy clusters are detected **only** via object-classification strings — NED
`Type` values `GClstr`/`GGroup`, plus `ClG`/`Cl`/`cluster` — caught by a regex and
then granted the 5 Mpc impact threshold (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:31-43,71-74`,
`engines.py:36-37`). There is **no dedicated cluster-catalog engine** (no WHL,
redMaPPer, SZ, or X-ray catalog) in the production path. WHL cross-matching exists
**only in exploratory notebooks** (`galaxies/v2_0/analysis.ipynb` ~46 WHL
references, `notebooks/codetections/interveners.ipynb`), not in `search.py`.
Consequence: **zero cluster-classified rows appear in any of the nine committed
`results/*_galaxies.csv`** — the 5 Mpc cluster search returns nothing in practice
for this sample. (Prior art below explains why this is expected at Dec > +70.)

### Other (non-production) search methods present in the tree
The repo carries three earlier/parallel search methods, useful as a record of
"methods tried":
- WISE-PS1-STRM + Legacy-DR8 photo-z **beam search** with an impact cut
  (`galaxies/wise-ps1-strm/wiseps1_beam_search.py`).
- Tarrío PS1 photo-z **SOV-tile impact matching** + Gaia cross-match over exactly
  the 12 targets (`galaxies/tarrio_ps1_photz/find_impact_matches.py`).
- A DESI **DR8 sweep+photo-z stack built specifically for the +70°–+75° Dec strip**
  (`galaxies/dr8_dec70_75/stack.py`) — a direct adaptation to the sample's
  high-declination band.

### Search → DM/τ budget chain
The found galaxies feed a documented, primary-source-citing physics chain
(`build_unified.py` → `scattering_predict.py` → `sightline_budget.py`):
- **Stellar-mass ladder** GLADE → 2MASS-K → DESI-SED → WISE-W1 → PS1-Taylor →
  assumed 10¹⁰ M⊙ (`galaxies/v2_0/build_unified.py:26-33,118-155`); halo mass via
  abundance matching, then R_vir / r_s / c (`build_unified.py:266-277`).
- **mNFW hot-halo DM** at the projected impact parameter (Prochaska & Zheng 2019;
  Macquart+2020) (`galaxies/v2_0/scattering_predict.py:27-123`).
- **Cool-CGM DM** scaled by a covering fraction and an MgII-equivalent-width proxy
  (Lan & Mo 2018; Nielsen+2013) (`scattering_predict.py:126-150,321-384`).
- **Two-phase intervening τ**: smooth hot halo + clumpy cool CGM carrying a
  fluctuation-parameter boost `COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` (McCourt+2018; Vedantham &
  Phinney 2019; Ocker+2021) (`scattering_predict.py:153-300`).
- **Intervening-screen geometric leverage** `g_scatt = D_L·D_LS/D_S/(1+z_L)²`
  (cited to Macquart & Koay 2013) — zero for a screen at/behind the source
  (`scattering_predict.py:192-217`).
- **Budget closure** DM_host = DM_obs − DM_MW,ISM(NE2001) − DM_MW,halo(40,
  Yamasaki & Totani 2020) − DM_cosmic(Macquart) − DM_interv
  (`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:124-143,86,376-608`), with a
  core-extrapolation cap that re-evaluates the hot column at b ≥ 0.1 R_vir
  (`sightline_budget.py:71,463-472`).
- **Prior-predictive sensitivity** over three literature-anchored prior families
  (`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_sensitivity.py`,
  `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-sightline-budget-sensitivity.md`).

### Actual per-sightline distribution (committed artifacts)
From `results/search_summary.csv` and `results/sightline_dm_scattering_budget.md`
(9/12 sightlines have committed `*_galaxies.csv`; matches the science plan's "host
search 9/12"):

| Sightline | z | N foreground gal. | N clusters | DM_interv (capped) | regime | predicted τ_interv (ms) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Zach | 0.043 | 2 | 0 | 143 | CGM | 0.006 |
| Whitney | 0.479 | 2 | 0 | 200 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 0.27 |
| Oran | 0.300 | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Isha | 0.251 | 1 | 0 | 23 | CGM | 0.0015 |
| Wilhelm | 0.510 | 2 | 0 | 103 | CGM | 0.008 |
| Phineas | 0.271 | 3 | 0 | 367 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 1.4 |
| Freya | 1.000 (placeholder) | 1 | 0 | 5 | CGM | 3.5e-05 |
| Hamilton | 0.302 | 1 | 0 | 58 | CGM | 0.022 |
| Mahi | 1.000 (placeholder) | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Chromatica | 0.074 | 2 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Casey | 0.287 | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Johndoeii | 1.000 (placeholder) | 1 | 0 | 213 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 0.23 |

Observations: (1) **No clusters found on any sightline.** (2) Foreground galaxy
counts are low (0–3), consistent with a shallow high-Dec catalog floor. (3) The
biggest intervening-DM cases (Phineas, Whitney, Johndoeii) are flagged
`GALAXY_INTERIOR` — the sightline passes inside 0.1 R_vir, so the raw mNFW column is
core-extrapolated and the capped value is the trustworthy one. (4) **Negative host
DM** on Zach/Whitney/Wilhelm/Phineas — under-dense sightlines that fall below the
Macquart cosmic mean (`sightline_budget.py:356-368`), already diagnosed in the
science plan as expected scatter, not a bug. (5) The code's own verdict is that
**predicted intervening τ is negligible everywhere except Phineas (1.4 ms)** — i.e.
intervening galaxies rarely dominate scattering in this sample.

## Prior Art

External pass run via direct arXiv/ar5iv full-text reads and primary-source web
search (the semantic search backend was rate-limited; every ID below was verified
against the primary source except where flagged *(verify)*).

### A. Identifying intervening/foreground galaxies along FRB sightlines
- The standard association cut is **impact parameter b⊥ ≲ r200 with a spec-z**;
  FLIMFLAM operationalizes this with deep narrow-field spectroscopy of galaxies near
  the sightline plus wide-field spectroscopy for the larger-scale density field
  (Khrykin, Lee, Simha et al. 2024, FLIMFLAM DR1, arXiv:2402.00505).
- **FLIMFLAM is the canonical end-to-end pipeline**: it reconstructs the ~Mpc matter
  density field along each sightline (Bayesian ARGO reconstruction), associates
  halos, and fits a DM model with MW/IGM/halo/host terms, then constrains the
  CGM/IGM baryon partition — f_IGM = 0.59 (+0.11/−0.10), CGM gas fraction f_gas =
  0.55 (+0.26/−0.29) (Khrykin et al. 2024, arXiv:2402.00505; method precursors
  Simha et al. 2020 arXiv:2005.13157, 2021 arXiv:2108.09881; Khrykin et al. 2023
  arXiv:2303.07387).
- **Baseband/interferometric foreground searches** are the DSA-110 mode: the
  first DSA-110 FRB+host catalog (Law/Sharma et al. 2023, arXiv:2307.03344) and the
  multi-halo burst FRB 20221219A, whose sightline is "significantly overcrowded"
  (CDF ≈ 0.99 vs expected N_halos = 0.18) with two intervening halos identified by
  optical/IR SED fitting (Connor et al. 2024, arXiv:2405.14182).
- **Statistical host/intervener association**: PATH Bayesian framework (Aggarwal et
  al. 2021, arXiv:2102.10627), with chance-coincidence sub-samples (Bhandari et al.
  2022, arXiv:2009.10747).
- The **r200 cut is itself a modeling choice**: Prochaska & Zheng 2019 warn the
  virial "edge" is artificial — integrating to 2·r200 adds tens of pc cm⁻³ at b ≳
  0.5 r200 (arXiv:1901.11051). The code's behavior here is the inverse problem (it
  caps at 0.1 R_vir for the inner blow-up) but the outer-truncation sensitivity is
  unhandled.

### B. Galaxy clusters along sightlines, and high-Dec catalog coverage
- **Cluster catalogs and their declination reach** (the binding constraint for this
  sample): WHL (Wen, Han & Liu 2012, arXiv:1202.6424; update Wen & Han 2015,
  arXiv:1503.04332 *(verify)*) and redMaPPer (Rykoff et al. 2014, arXiv:1303.3562)
  are **SDSS-based and do not cover Dec > +70** (SDSS imaging in the N Galactic Cap
  ends near +60–65°). SZ catalogs ACT DR5 (Hilton et al. 2021, arXiv:2009.11043)
  and SPT-SZ (Bleem et al. 2015, arXiv:1409.0850), and X-ray eROSITA eRASS1 (Bulbul
  et al. 2024, A&A 685,A106; cosmology companion arXiv:2402.08458) are
  equatorial/southern. **The only catalogs with genuine Dec > +70 coverage are the
  all-sky ones: Planck PSZ2 (SZ; Planck 2016, arXiv:1502.01598) and MCXC /
  MCXC-II (X-ray RASS-based; Piffaretti et al. 2011, arXiv:1007.1916; MCXC-II
  arXiv:2402.01538)** — both shallow and mass-limited there, so poor completeness
  for group-mass systems.
- **FRBs through clusters give meaningful DM but negligible scattering.** DSA-110
  FRBs in Abell 2310/2311 contribute ICM DM of 16–568 pc cm⁻³ but show no
  significant scattering (Connor et al. 2023, arXiv:2302.14788). FRB 20190520B
  pierces two foreground clusters (b = 0.39 and 0.77 r200) contributing 450–640 pc
  cm⁻³ total halo DM, and the authors explicitly argue these clusters do **not**
  cause the burst's large τ (Lee et al. 2023, arXiv:2306.05403).
- **DM vs impact parameter is steep**: ~300 pc cm⁻³ at 0.39 r200 falling to ~110 pc
  cm⁻³ at 0.77 r200 (Lee 2023). Meaningful cluster DM requires the sightline within
  ~1–2 r200 (≲ a few Mpc for the most massive clusters); a **5 Mpc impact radius
  mostly returns negligible-DM near-misses** (this last step is an extrapolation
  from the observed profiles, not a directly stated literature result).
- Cluster turbulent ICM can in principle enhance scattering (Zhu/Feng et al. 2018,
  arXiv:1808.05739), but geometric leverage for a cosmologically-distant cluster
  screen drives broadening to ≪ ms — negligible vs FRB τ (Ocker et al. 2022,
  arXiv:2203.16716, secondary).

### C. CGM/halo gas → DM and scattering models
- **mNFW gas profile** ρ_b(r) ∝ 1/[y^(1−α)(y0+y)^(2+α)], fiducial y0 = α = 2, f_b,halo
  = 0.75 (Prochaska & Zheng 2019, arXiv:1901.11051) — **exactly the form the code
  implements and cites** (`scattering_predict.py:27-123`). FLIMFLAM uses the same
  form.
- **Macquart relation** ⟨DM_cosmic⟩ ∝ ∫ f_IGM(1+z)/E(z) dz (Macquart et al. 2020,
  Nature 581, arXiv:2005.13161 — verified) — the baseline the code uses for cosmic
  DM (`sightline_budget.py:124-143`).
- **Geometric leverage**: τ ≈ 48.03 μs · A_τ F̃ DM_ℓ² G_scatt /[(1+z_ℓ)³ ν⁴], with
  G_scatt = 2 d_sl d_lo /(L d_so) (Ocker, Cordes & Chatterjee 2021,
  arXiv:2101.04784). This is the canonical FRB-halo leverage paper; the code cites
  Macquart & Koay 2013 for the equivalent geometry (`scattering_predict.py:192-217`)
  and could additionally cite Ocker+2021.
- **Cool-CGM cloudlet scattering**: foundational model is Vedantham & Phinney 2019
  (arXiv:1811.10876) on McCourt+2018 "shattered" sub-pc cloudlets; refractive
  cloudlet lensing (arXiv:2309.07256) — see citation caveat in Synthesis. But
  **CGM cloud-size limits** suggest realistic low-z cool CGM (n_e ≲ 10⁻² cm⁻³) adds
  little scattering unless clouds are ≲ 0.1 pc (arXiv:2504.19562), and hot-CGM
  scattering is ≪ 1 μs at 1 GHz (Ocker et al. 2025, arXiv:2503.02329). The code's
  `COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` (range 3–30) is therefore an **optimistic** cool-phase
  prior (`scattering_predict.py:254-262`).

### D. MgII absorbers as intervening cool-gas tracers
- **No published work ties an individual MgII absorber to a measured FRB DM or τ
  excess on the same sightline** — this is a genuine gap (a claimed link from a
  secondary source produced only hallucinated IDs and was discarded). MgII↔cool-CGM
  baseline relations are quasar-based: W(MgII 2796) declines with impact parameter,
  high covering fraction within ~100–150 kpc of L* galaxies (MAGIICAT — Nielsen et
  al. 2013; Lan & Mo 2018) — the relations the code's `predict_mgii_wr` and
  `cool_covering_fraction` encode (`scattering_predict.py:321-384`). MgII in
  groups/clusters: Anand et al. 2022 (arXiv:2201.07811). The DSA multi-halo burst
  uses COS-Halos N_HII(b,M⋆) — the same cool photoionized phase MgII traces — as its
  DM_igh template (arXiv:2405.14182).

### E. Disconfirming evidence & limitations
- **Scattering is usually host/Milky-Way-dominated, not foreground-dominated.**
  Halos contribute to DM "but evidently not to scattering, which is dominated by
  host galaxies" (Cordes et al. 2022, arXiv:2108.01172); measured intervening-halo
  τ is negligible for FRB 181112/191108 (Ocker et al. 2021, arXiv:2101.04784); FRB
  20190520B is explicitly host-dominated (Ocker et al. 2022, arXiv:2202.13458);
  CHIME population modeling concurs (arXiv:2502.05838). **Counterpoint:** the DSA
  multi-halo burst FRB 20221219A is *not* host-dominated — an overcrowded
  intervening sightline can dominate (Connor et al. 2024, arXiv:2405.14182). That is
  precisely the regime the co-detection sample is built to probe.
- **High-Dec photo-z incompleteness / catalog depth.** DESI Legacy photo-z degrade
  where spec-z training is sparse — directly the Dec > +70 case (Duncan 2022,
  arXiv:2203.01949; Zhou 2021, arXiv:2001.06018; SOM analyses arXiv:2409.14799). A
  Rubin-era forecast finds the dominant error is **missing dim/high-z hosts**
  (catalog depth), degrading inferences ~47–62%, far more than photo-z scatter
  (~7%) (arXiv:2603.00371). SDSS spectroscopy largely excludes Dec > +70; GLADE+ is
  shallow; PS1 lacks spectroscopy — so the project's high-Dec search is
  photometry-limited and spec-z-starved. No paper quantifies FRB-foreground
  completeness specifically at Dec > +70 (gap).
- **False-association risk.** PATH quantifies chance coincidence (arXiv:2102.10627);
  host association degrades once σ_loc ≳ 2″ (arXiv:2502.16791), so large-impact
  foreground association under coarse localization or photo-z-only redshifts is
  fragile. No FRB-specific paper gives a contamination number for intervening (vs
  host) association under photo-z only (gap).

## Synthesis

**The code implements a defensible, literature-grounded version of the standard
method, but a deliberately simplified one.** Its impact-parameter + (photo-z or
spec-z) cone search, mNFW halo DM, Macquart cosmic baseline, two-phase cool/hot
screen, and geometric leverage all map directly onto the canonical references
(Prochaska & Zheng 2019, Macquart+2020, Ocker+2021, FLIMFLAM), and the modules cite
them inline. What it does **not** do — and what FLIMFLAM does — is reconstruct the
Mpc-scale density field or jointly fit the baryon partition; the code instead
attributes per-halo columns deterministically and brackets the nuisance
astrophysics with a prior-predictive sensitivity layer. That is an appropriate
scope choice for a 12-sightline study, not a defect.

**The external consensus validates the code's central scientific verdict.** Across
Cordes 2022, Ocker 2021/2022, and CHIME population work, intervening halos add DM
but rarely scattering — exactly what the budget table reports (predicted τ_interv
negligible everywhere except Phineas). The one regime where this flips —
overcrowded sightlines (DSA multi-halo, arXiv:2405.14182) — is the regime this
sample targets, so the scientific payoff lives in the tail, and Phineas (3
foreground galaxies, GALAXY_INTERIOR, τ_interv = 1.4 ms) is the natural first case
to scrutinize.

**The cluster search is the clearest weakness, and prior art explains why.**
Clusters are found only by NED object-type strings with a 5 Mpc impact, and **zero
clusters turn up** on any sightline. Two independent reasons: (1) at Dec +70°–74°,
the SDSS-based cluster catalogs (WHL, redMaPPer) have no coverage, and even the
notebook WHL cross-match cannot help there — only all-sky Planck PSZ2 and
MCXC/MCXC-II reach the band, both shallow; (2) a 5 Mpc impact mostly yields
negligible-DM near-misses, because meaningful cluster DM needs the sightline within
~1–2 r200, and clusters contribute essentially no scattering at any impact (Connor
2023, Lee 2023). So the current cluster machinery is both under-powered (wrong
catalogs for this sky) and aimed at a low-yield regime.

### Gaps and open questions
- **Cluster catalog coverage at Dec > +70.** If clusters matter, the production
  search needs an all-sky catalog engine (Planck PSZ2 and/or MCXC-II), not NED-type
  strings — and the 5 Mpc radius should be re-examined against an r200-relative
  criterion. *(Light recommendation; design deferred to planning.)*
- **`COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` is optimistic.** Recent cloud-size/turbulence limits
  (arXiv:2504.19562, arXiv:2503.02329) push the realistic cool-CGM scattering
  contribution down; the lo bound (3) may be closer to reality. Worth a sensitivity
  knob already partly present in `sightline_sensitivity.py`.
- **MgII → FRB DM/τ is an unfilled gap in the literature.** The code already
  predicts MgII W_r and uses it to boost cool DM, but that proxy has **no direct
  FRB observational validation** anywhere — it is a modeling assumption, and a
  potential novelty angle if the sample can test it. Flag clearly in any write-up.
- **Outer-truncation (2·r200) DM sensitivity** is unhandled; Prochaska & Zheng warn
  it adds tens of pc cm⁻³ at moderate impact.
- **Citation to verify before publication.** `docs/codetection-science-plan.md:64`
  cites "Sammons 2309.07256" for CGM cloudlet lensing; the prior-art pass confirms
  arXiv:2309.07256 is the cloudlet refractive-lensing paper but reports its lead
  author is **not** Sammons. A separate Sammons et al. FRB-scattering paper may
  exist. Re-check the author/ID pairing via an ADS author search before this
  citation reaches a manuscript. *(Left in place pending confirmation; not silently
  edited because the correct attribution was not established this pass.)*
- **arXiv IDs flagged *(verify)*:** Wen & Han 2015 (1503.04332), eROSITA eRASS1
  catalog-description paper (companion 2402.08458 confirmed). Journal refs reliable;
  confirm exact arXiv numbers before citing.

## References / Sources

### Code (commit `8ca87f7`)
- `galaxies/v2_0/config.py:9-17,21-56` — targets, catalogs, impact/redshift defaults
- `galaxies/v2_0/search.py:31-43,56-74,87-201,204-332` — cluster regex, foreground
  mask, dedup, PS1 backfill, search driver
- `galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:23-148` — NED / Vizier / PS1 engines
- `galaxies/v2_0/engines_extra.py` — opt-in DESI DR1 spec-z engine
- `galaxies/v2_0/enrichers.py` — opt-in cross-survey enrichment
- `galaxies/v2_0/build_unified.py:26-33,118-155,246-474` — mass ladder, per-galaxy
  CGM record assembly
- `galaxies/v2_0/scattering_predict.py:27-385` — mNFW DM, cool DM, MgII W_r,
  covering fraction, two-phase τ, g_scatt
- `galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:71-143,340-608` — DM/τ budget closure, caps,
  verdicts
- `galaxies/v2_0/sightline_sensitivity.py` — prior-predictive sensitivity layer
- `galaxies/wise-ps1-strm/wiseps1_beam_search.py`,
  `galaxies/tarrio_ps1_photz/find_impact_matches.py`,
  `galaxies/dr8_dec70_75/stack.py` — non-production / earlier search methods
- `notebooks/codetections/interveners.ipynb`, `galaxies/v2_0/analysis.ipynb` —
  exploratory WHL cluster cross-match (notebook-only)
- `results/search_summary.csv`, `results/sightline_dm_scattering_budget.md`,
  `results/*_galaxies.csv` — committed per-sightline outputs

### External (verified arXiv IDs unless flagged)
- Prochaska & Zheng 2019, mNFW halo gas/DM — arXiv:1901.11051 *(verify exact ID)*
- Macquart et al. 2020, Nature 581, Macquart relation — arXiv:2005.13161

  formalism Cordes et al. 2016 — arXiv:1605.05890

# Prior-Art Pass: FRB Foreground Galaxy / CGM Methods

All arXiv IDs were returned by the semantic paper-search backend or independently verified. Two attribution issues flagged inline: (i) the brief's "Sammons et al. 2023, arXiv:2309.07256" — that ID is the *cloudlet refractive-lensing* paper (Swinburne group; lead author is NOT Sammons). Treat as misattributed. (ii) Perplexity hallucinated several MgII/PATH arXiv IDs — discarded and replaced with verified ones.

## A. Methods to identify intervening/foreground galaxies along FRB sightlines

- Impact-parameter + spec-z association at b⊥ ≲ r200 is the standard CGM-association cut. FLIMFLAM uses deeper narrow-field spectroscopy of galaxies at b⊥ ≲ r200, separate from wide-field spectroscopy for the IGM density field (Khrykin/Lee/Simha et al. 2024, arXiv:2402.00505).
- "FRB foreground mapping" / DM-partition technique: FLIMFLAM DR1 reconstructs the ~Mpc-scale matter density field along 8 sightlines (ARGO Bayesian reconstruction) + halo associations, fitting a DM model with MW/IGM/halo/host terms — the canonical end-to-end pipeline (arXiv:2402.00505). Forecast precursors: Lee et al. 2022 + Simha et al. 2020 (foreground spectroscopy cuts cosmic-variance scatter ×2–3).
- FLIMFLAM constrains CGM/IGM baryon partition (feedback proxy): f_igm = 0.59 (+0.11/−0.10), CGM gas fraction f_gas = 0.55 (+0.26/−0.29) for 10^10–10^13 M⊙ halos (f_cgm = 0.20 of cosmic baryons in CGM); first systematic split of host DM into halo vs ISM/engine. Feedback prescriptions strongly change the partition (Khrykin et al. 2023). (arXiv:2402.00505)
- Per-sightline excess-DM attribution to specific halos/groups: Khrykin et al. 2023 (arXiv:2303.07387, 4 FRBs); Simha 2020 (arXiv:2005.13157, FRB 190608); Simha 2021 (arXiv:2108.09881, FRB 180924).
- Group/cluster sightline crowding: FRB 20190520B intersects foreground groups/clusters, 701 redshifts (Lee 2023, arXiv:2306.05403). FRB 20221219A (DSA-110) sightline "significantly overcrowded" (CDF ~0.99 vs expected N_halos = 0.18), two intervening halos (Connor 2024, arXiv:2405.14182).
- Baseband/interferometric foreground searches — DSA-110: first FRB+host catalog, 11 FRBs (Law/Sharma 2023, arXiv:2307.03344). Multi-halo burst (arXiv:2405.14182) identifies two interveners at z=0.492 (b=43 kpc) and z=0.438 (b=36 kpc) via optical/IR + SED fitting, then DM_igh ≈ 10 & 14 pc cm⁻³ from COS-Halos N_HII(b,M⋆).
- Host-association statistical framework: PATH (Aggarwal et al. 2021, arXiv:2102.10627) — Bayesian P(O_i|x) from coords+uncertainties, fluxes, angular sizes. Extensions: PATH+host models (arXiv:2606.10538); chance-coincidence sub-sample (Bhandari 2022, arXiv:2009.10747).
- Virial-radius/impact-parameter cut originates in Prochaska & Zheng 2019 — which warns the r200 "edge" is artificial: integrating to 2·r200 adds tens of pc cm⁻³ at b ≳ 0.5 r200. The cut radius is itself a modeling choice affecting DM/τ budgets.

## C. CGM / halo-gas → DM and scattering (τ) models

mNFW gas profile (Prochaska & Zheng 2019, arXiv:1901.11051):
- ρ_b(r) = ρ_b^0 / [y^(1−α)(y0+y)^(2+α)], y ≡ c(r/r200); fiducial y0 = 2, α = 2. NFW disfavored for gas (too-high inner DM/short cooling time); mNFW flattens the core.
- Fiducial f_b,halo = 0.75 (25% of baryons in stars/ISM). DM_MW,halo ≈ 50–80 pc cm⁻³ to 200 kpc; a 10^12 M⊙ halo gives tens of pc cm⁻³. FLIMFLAM uses this exact form (y0=α=2), f_gas free, stellar→halo via Moster 2013. Reference code: github.com/FRBs.

Macquart relation (Macquart et al. 2020, Nature 581; arXiv:2005.13161 — verified):
- ⟨DM_cosmic⟩ ∝ ∫ f_IGM(1+z)/E(z) dz (~935·f_IGM·I(z) pc cm⁻³; functional form McQuinn 2014/Inoue 2004, Macquart is the observational confirmation). The baseline against which "excess" DM is attributed to foreground halos.

Scattering from intervening halos / CGM cloudlets:
- Vedantham & Phinney 2019 (arXiv:1811.10876, MNRAS 483, 971 — verified): scattering by cool (~10^4 K) CGM clumps, on the McCourt 2018 "fog"/shattering model (sub-pc cloudlets, N_e ~ 10^17 cm⁻²). Foundational.
- Cloudlet refractive lensing (arXiv:2309.07256 — "Sammons 2023" in brief is MISATTRIBUTED; this is the sub-pc-cloudlet lensing paper, Swinburne group): an FRB within r200 of a galaxy is lensed into ~20 refractive images, ~10.5 ms lensing timescale (f_V^200=10⁻⁴, r200=100 kpc, N_e^c=10^17 cm⁻², r_c=0.1 pc, 1 GHz); single-cloudlet τ ~ 35 μs. Such cloudlets would *suppress* scintillation.
- Ocker, Cordes & Chatterjee 2021 (arXiv:2101.04784 — verified): THE geometric-leverage paper. τ ≈ 48.03 μs · A_τ F̃ DM_ℓ² / [(1+z_ℓ)³ ν⁴] · G_scatt, with G_scatt = 2 d_sl d_lo / (L d_so) for a thin layer — the lever-arm boost requested. Finds F̃ ≥10× smaller in halos than disk; MW-halo τ ≲ 12 μs at 1 GHz; intervening-halo limits for FRB 181112 (≲21 μs) & 191108 (≲4 ns) → halos make negligible scattering for those sightlines. BUT explicitly: an intervening halo CAN amplify scattering ×100+ vs the MW halo via leverage; host still often dominates.
- Cordes et al. 2022 (arXiv:2108.01172 — verified): 14 FRBs; IGM+halos contribute to DM "but evidently not to scattering, which is then dominated by host galaxies." Uses τ as a host-DM + redshift estimator. Scattering formalism (w/ Cordes 2016, arXiv:1605.05890) reused by the DSA multi-halo paper.
- Ocker et al. 2025, CGM turbulence microphysics (arXiv:2503.02329): hot-CGM scattering negligible (≪1 μs at 1 GHz); cool CGM detectable depending on geometry.
- Cosmic-web scattering (arXiv:2603.22108): sheets/filaments negligible; turbulent cloudlets in intervening massive-halo CGM detectable on rare sightlines. Companion: arXiv:2603.00336.
- CGM cloud-size limits from refractive scattering (arXiv:2504.19562): bulk of low-z cool CGM (n_e ≲ 10⁻² cm⁻³) likely cannot produce two refractive images / scattering unless clouds ≲0.1 pc — disconfirming-leaning.

## D. MgII absorbers as intervening cool-gas tracers for FRB DM/scattering

- Direct MgII→FRB-DM/scattering links are essentially UNCONFIRMED. No published paper ties an individual MgII absorber to a measured FRB DM or τ excess on the same sightline. Perplexity asserted such work but produced only hallucinated IDs — treat MgII→FRB as a GAP, not prior art. Potential novelty angle for the team.
- MgII as a cool-CGM tracer (established, quasar-based, not FRB): W(MgII λ2796) declines with impact parameter; strong absorbers cluster at small b; covering fraction high within ~100–150 kpc of L* galaxies (MAGIICAT — Nielsen 2013; Chen 2010; Lan & Fukugita 2018). (Secondary-level synthesis; individual MAGIICAT IDs not separately verified this pass.)
- MgII in clusters / DESI×SDSS: Anand et al. 2022 (arXiv:2201.07811), covering fractions ~4–5× random. MEGAFLOW X (arXiv:2312.01762) for groups.
- Closest in spirit (not MgII): the DSA multi-halo paper (arXiv:2405.14182) uses COS-Halos N_HII(b,M⋆) — same cool photoionized phase MgII traces — for DM_igh. The practical "cool-CGM → DM" template to adapt to MgII.

## E. Disconfirming evidence & limitations

Scattering usually host-/MW-dominated, not foreground-dominated:
- Cordes 2022 (arXiv:2108.01172): halos contribute to DM "but evidently not to scattering, which is dominated by host galaxies."
- Ocker 2021 (arXiv:2101.04784): measured intervening-halo τ negligible for 181112/191108; F̃ ≥10× below disk. (Caveat: leverage can boost ×100 in favorable geometry.)
- FRB 20190520B (Ocker 2022, arXiv:2202.13458): "large dispersion and scattering dominated by the host galaxy" — explicit host-dominated counterexample. Circumsource variability: arXiv:2210.01975.
- CHIME population modeling (arXiv:2502.05838, arXiv:2107.10858): scattering predominantly host/circumsource.
- CGM cloud-size limits (arXiv:2504.19562): realistic intervening CGM adds little scattering.
- COUNTERPOINT for balance: the DSA multi-halo burst (arXiv:2405.14182) concludes the host does NOT dominate scattering for FRB 20221219A — i.e., overcrowded intervening sightlines CAN dominate, presented as the exception requiring sightline overcrowding. This is exactly the regime the high-Dec co-detection sample probes.

Photo-z incompleteness / catalog depth, esp. high Dec:
- DESI Legacy photo-z (Duncan 2022, arXiv:2203.01949; Zhou 2021, arXiv:2001.06018, σ_NMAD~0.02 for LRGs) degrade at faint mags; spec-z training sparse at high Dec / faint end. SOM analyses (arXiv:2409.14799, arXiv:2207.08657): photo-z unreliable where galaxies under-represented in training set — directly relevant to Dec > +70.
- FRBs with photo-z only (arXiv:2603.00371, Rubin-era forecast): photo-z errors degrade H0 ~7%, but missing dim/high-z hosts degrade ~47–62% — dominant high-Dec risk is catalog DEPTH / missing faint foreground galaxies, not photo-z scatter.
- SDSS spectroscopy largely excludes Dec > +70; GLADE+ shallow; Pan-STARRS lacks spectroscopy. A high-Dec foreground search is photometry-limited and spec-z-starved — real, citable, though no paper quantifies FRB-foreground completeness specifically at Dec > +70 (gap).

False-association risk:
- PATH (arXiv:2102.10627) quantifies chance coincidence; localization-precision bias (arXiv:2502.16791) shows hosts stable until σ_loc ≳ 2″, then degrade — large-b foreground association with coarse localization is fragile.
- Selection biases (Seebeck/Shannon 2021, arXiv:2112.07639): radio selection (high-DM/low-scattering) skews the sample, biasing foreground-incidence stats.
- No single FRB-specific paper gives a contamination number for intervening (vs host) association under photo-z only (gap).

## Key papers (deduplicated, verified arXiv IDs)
- Prochaska & Zheng 2019 (mNFW) — arXiv:1901.11051
- Macquart 2020 (Nature; Macquart relation) — arXiv:2005.13161
- Khrykin/Lee/Simha 2024 (FLIMFLAM DR1) — arXiv:2402.00505
- Khrykin 2023 (FLIMFLAM excess-DM) — arXiv:2303.07387
- Lee 2023 (FRB 20190520B foreground clusters) — arXiv:2306.05403
- Simha 2020 (FRB 190608) — arXiv:2005.13157; Simha 2021 (FRB 180924) — arXiv:2108.09881
- Ocker, Cordes & Chatterjee 2021 (halo scattering + G_scatt leverage) — arXiv:2101.04784
- Cordes 2022 (DM/τ, host-dominated scattering) — arXiv:2108.01172
- Vedantham & Phinney 2019 (CGM cool-clump scattering) — arXiv:1811.10876
- Cloudlet refractive lensing (~20 images, ~10.5 ms; "Sammons 2023" misattributed) — arXiv:2309.07256
- Ocker 2025 (CGM turbulence microphysics) — arXiv:2503.02329
- CGM cloud-size limits — arXiv:2504.19562
- Cosmic-web scattering — arXiv:2603.22108; CGM scattering morphology — arXiv:2603.00336
- Connor 2024 (DSA-110 multi-halo FRB 20221219A) — arXiv:2405.14182
- Law/Sharma 2023 (DSA-110 first FRB+host catalog) — arXiv:2307.03344
- Ocker 2022 (FRB 20190520B host-dominated) — arXiv:2202.13458
- Aggarwal 2021 (PATH) — arXiv:2102.10627
- Seebeck/Shannon 2021 (FRB selection biases) — arXiv:2112.07639; localization-precision bias — arXiv:2502.16791
- Duncan 2022 (Legacy DR8 photo-z) — arXiv:2203.01949; Rubin-era FRB photo-z impact — arXiv:2603.00371
- Cordes 2016 (scattering formalism) — arXiv:1605.05890; Radio scattering horizons (Ocker 2022) — arXiv:2203.16716

## Gaps / could not confirm
- MgII → FRB DM/scattering: NO verified primary source. MgII–CGM baseline relations (MAGIICAT) exist but were synthesized from secondary sources; the FRB-specific link is a genuine gap — a potential novelty angle for the team.
- arXiv:2309.07256 authorship: confirmed as the cloudlet refractive-lensing paper (Swinburne group) but lead author is NOT Sammons — the brief's citation key looks wrong; re-check before publication. A separate Sammons et al. FRB-scattering-as-cosmological-probe paper may exist but was not surfaced — worth a targeted ADS author search.
- High-Dec (>+70°) FRB-foreground completeness: no paper quantifies catalog completeness for FRB sightlines specifically at Dec > +70; the spec-z-starvation argument is sound but uncited at that specificity — state as the team's own assessment.
- Quantified photo-z-only false-positive rate for intervening (vs host) association: reasoned in the literature but no single number — gap.
</agent-message>

input.content:
# Research: Foreground galaxy & galaxy-cluster distributions along the co-detected sightlines, and the methods used to search for them

**Date:** 2026-06-23
**Scope:** both internal codebase and external prior art
**Codebase state researched against:** commit `8ca87f7`, 2026-06-23 (branch `feature/radiometer-flux-cal`)
**Related Documents:**
- `docs/codetection-science-plan.md` (the parent science plan; §B/§D ground this question)
- `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-sightline-budget-sensitivity.md`
- `CONTEXT.md`, `docs/adr/0001-two-band-leverage-positioning.md`

**Citation note:** inline references use author-year; full arXiv IDs are in
*References* below. IDs marked `[unverified]` there were reported as
read/verified by the prior-art sub-agents but could **not** be re-confirmed by the
local citation-check hook this session (network unreachable, no cache). Confirm
those IDs against ADS/arXiv before any reach a manuscript.

## Question / Scope

For each of the 12 CHIME/DSA co-detected FRB sightlines, what foreground galaxies
and galaxy clusters lie along the line of sight, and what methods does the project
(and the wider literature) use to search for them? In scope: the production
foreground-search machinery, the search→DM/scattering-budget chain it feeds, the
actual per-sightline distributions currently committed, and the prior-art methods
for (a) intervening-galaxy identification, (b) cluster-intersection searches, and
(c) turning a foreground system into a DM/τ contribution. Out of scope: the
scattering-fit pipeline itself, host-galaxy (z ≥ z_FRB) association, and the
flux-calibration work on the current branch.

A defining property of the sample shapes everything below: **all 12 sightlines sit
at declination +70° to +74°** (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:21-34`), a sky region where
the standard optical survey footprints (SDSS, and the catalogs built on it) thin
out or vanish.

## Codebase Findings

### The sample
12 sightlines, Dec +70° to +74°, z from 0.043 to 0.51, with **three placeholder
z = 1.0** values (Freya, Mahi, Johndoeii) that flag an unmeasured host redshift
rather than a real one (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:21-34`,
`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:73-81`). Where z is a placeholder the cosmic and
host DM terms are withheld rather than fabricated
(`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:504-515`).

### Production foreground search (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py`)
- **Engines / catalogs.** NED cone search (`galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:23-54`) plus
  Vizier for GLADE+ (`VII/291`), DESI DR8-North (`VII/292`), and SDSS DR12
  (`V/147`) (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:37-41`, `galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:57-102`).
  An opt-in DESI DR1 spec-z TAP engine covers only 3/12 targets
  (Whitney/Phineas/Casey) and is **off by default**
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:218-221`, `galaxies/v2_0/config.py:45,55`). Enricher
  catalogs (Legacy DR10 tractor, AllWISE, GALEX, 2MASS-XSC) are likewise off by
  default (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:48-56`, `galaxies/v2_0/enrichers.py`).
- **Foreground criterion.** A row is foreground if `z < z_FRB + 0.01`, with a
  photo-z-error-aware relaxation (`z − 2σ_z < z_limit`)
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:56-74`, `config.py:13`).
- **Impact-parameter cut.** 100 kpc for galaxies, **5 Mpc for clusters**
  (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:9-11`, `search.py:71-74`). The query cone is sized to
  the larger limit and capped at 2° so low-z cluster cones don't time out Vizier
  (`galaxies/v2_0/config.py:15-17`, `search.py:228-234`).
- **Dedup & photometry backfill.** Cross-catalog duplicates merged by 10″ + scaled
  Δz, keeping the smaller-photo-z-error / higher-rank source
  (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:87-149`); PS1 g/i magnitudes backfilled for rows
  lacking a catalog stellar mass — the only mass route for DESI-photo-z-only
  galaxies (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:152-201`, `engines.py:109-148`).

### Cluster handling — the main gap
Galaxy clusters are detected **only** via object-classification strings — NED
`Type` values `GClstr`/`GGroup`, plus `ClG`/`Cl`/`cluster` — caught by a regex and
then granted the 5 Mpc impact threshold (`galaxies/v2_0/search.py:31-43,71-74`,
`engines.py:36-37`). There is **no dedicated cluster-catalog engine** (no WHL,
redMaPPer, SZ, or X-ray catalog) in the production path. WHL cross-matching exists
**only in exploratory notebooks** (`galaxies/v2_0/analysis.ipynb` ~46 WHL
references, `notebooks/codetections/interveners.ipynb`), not in `search.py`.
Consequence: **zero cluster-classified rows appear in any of the nine committed
`results/*_galaxies.csv`** — the 5 Mpc cluster search returns nothing in practice
for this sample. (Prior art below explains why this is expected at Dec > +70.)

### Other (non-production) search methods present in the tree
The repo carries three earlier/parallel search methods, useful as a record of
"methods tried":
- WISE-PS1-STRM + Legacy-DR8 photo-z **beam search** with an impact cut
  (`galaxies/wise-ps1-strm/wiseps1_beam_search.py`).
- Tarrío PS1 photo-z **SOV-tile impact matching** + Gaia cross-match over exactly
  the 12 targets (`galaxies/tarrio_ps1_photz/find_impact_matches.py`).
- A DESI **DR8 sweep+photo-z stack built specifically for the +70°–+75° Dec strip**
  (`galaxies/dr8_dec70_75/stack.py`) — a direct adaptation to the sample's
  high-declination band.

### Search → DM/τ budget chain
The found galaxies feed a documented, primary-source-citing physics chain
(`build_unified.py` → `scattering_predict.py` → `sightline_budget.py`):
- **Stellar-mass ladder** GLADE → 2MASS-K → DESI-SED → WISE-W1 → PS1-Taylor →
  assumed 10¹⁰ M⊙ (`galaxies/v2_0/build_unified.py:26-33,118-155`); halo mass via
  abundance matching, then R_vir / r_s / c (`build_unified.py:266-277`).
- **mNFW hot-halo DM** at the projected impact parameter (Prochaska & Zheng 2019;
  Macquart et al. 2020) (`galaxies/v2_0/scattering_predict.py:27-123`).
- **Cool-CGM DM** scaled by a covering fraction and an MgII-equivalent-width proxy
  (Lan & Mo 2018; Nielsen et al. 2013) (`scattering_predict.py:126-150,321-384`).
- **Two-phase intervening τ**: smooth hot halo + clumpy cool CGM carrying a
  fluctuation-parameter boost `COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` (McCourt et al. 2018;
  Vedantham & Phinney 2019; Ocker et al. 2021) (`scattering_predict.py:153-300`).
- **Intervening-screen geometric leverage** `g_scatt = D_L·D_LS/D_S/(1+z_L)²`
  (cited to Macquart & Koay 2013) — zero for a screen at/behind the source
  (`scattering_predict.py:192-217`).
- **Budget closure** DM_host = DM_obs − DM_MW,ISM(NE2001) − DM_MW,halo(40,
  Yamasaki & Totani 2020) − DM_cosmic(Macquart) − DM_interv
  (`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:124-143,86,376-608`), with a
  core-extrapolation cap that re-evaluates the hot column at b ≥ 0.1 R_vir
  (`sightline_budget.py:71,463-472`).
- **Prior-predictive sensitivity** over three literature-anchored prior families
  (`galaxies/v2_0/sightline_sensitivity.py`,
  `docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-19-sightline-budget-sensitivity.md`).

### Actual per-sightline distribution (committed artifacts)
From `results/search_summary.csv` and `results/sightline_dm_scattering_budget.md`
(9/12 sightlines have committed `*_galaxies.csv`; matches the science plan's "host
search 9/12"):

| Sightline | z | N foreground gal. | N clusters | DM_interv (capped) | regime | predicted τ_interv (ms) |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| Zach | 0.043 | 2 | 0 | 143 | CGM | 0.006 |
| Whitney | 0.479 | 2 | 0 | 200 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 0.27 |
| Oran | 0.300 | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Isha | 0.251 | 1 | 0 | 23 | CGM | 0.0015 |
| Wilhelm | 0.510 | 2 | 0 | 103 | CGM | 0.008 |
| Phineas | 0.271 | 3 | 0 | 367 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 1.4 |
| Freya | 1.000 (placeholder) | 1 | 0 | 5 | CGM | 3.5e-05 |
| Hamilton | 0.302 | 1 | 0 | 58 | CGM | 0.022 |
| Mahi | 1.000 (placeholder) | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Chromatica | 0.074 | 2 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Casey | 0.287 | 0 | 0 | 0 | none | 0 |
| Johndoeii | 1.000 (placeholder) | 1 | 0 | 213 | GALAXY_INTERIOR | 0.23 |

Observations: (1) **No clusters found on any sightline.** (2) Foreground galaxy
counts are low (0–3), consistent with a shallow high-Dec catalog floor. (3) The
biggest intervening-DM cases (Phineas, Whitney, Johndoeii) are flagged
`GALAXY_INTERIOR` — the sightline passes inside 0.1 R_vir, so the raw mNFW column is
core-extrapolated and the capped value is the trustworthy one. (4) **Negative host
DM** on Zach/Whitney/Wilhelm/Phineas — under-dense sightlines that fall below the
Macquart cosmic mean (`sightline_budget.py:356-368`), already diagnosed in the
science plan as expected scatter, not a bug. (5) The code's own verdict is that
**predicted intervening τ is negligible everywhere except Phineas (1.4 ms)** — i.e.
intervening galaxies rarely dominate scattering in this sample.

## Prior Art

External pass run via direct arXiv/ar5iv full-text reads and primary-source web
search (the semantic search backend was rate-limited; the sub-agents report that
every ID below was verified against the primary source except where flagged
`[unverified]` in *References*, which only means the local hook could not
re-confirm offline this session).

### A. Identifying intervening/foreground galaxies along FRB sightlines
- The standard association cut is **impact parameter b⊥ ≲ r200 with a spec-z**;
  FLIMFLAM operationalizes this with deep narrow-field spectroscopy of galaxies near
  the sightline plus wide-field spectroscopy for the larger-scale density field
  (Khrykin, Lee, Simha et al. 2024, FLIMFLAM DR1).
- **FLIMFLAM is the canonical end-to-end pipeline**: it reconstructs the ~Mpc matter
  density field along each sightline (Bayesian ARGO reconstruction), associates
  halos, and fits a DM model with MW/IGM/halo/host terms, then constrains the
  CGM/IGM baryon partition — f_IGM = 0.59 (+0.11/−0.10), CGM gas fraction f_gas =
  0.55 (+0.26/−0.29) (Khrykin, Lee, Simha et al. 2024; method precursors Simha et
  al. 2020, 2021; Khrykin et al. 2023).
- **Baseband/interferometric foreground searches** are the DSA-110 mode: the
  first DSA-110 FRB+host catalog (Law, Sharma et al. 2023) and the multi-halo burst
  FRB 20221219A, whose sightline is "significantly overcrowded" (CDF ≈ 0.99 vs
  expected N_halos = 0.18) with two intervening halos identified by optical/IR SED
  fitting (Connor et al. 2024).
- **Statistical host/intervener association**: PATH Bayesian framework (Aggarwal et
  al. 2021), with chance-coincidence sub-samples (Bhandari et al. 2022).
- The **r200 cut is itself a modeling choice**: Prochaska & Zheng 2019 warn the
  virial "edge" is artificial — integrating to 2·r200 adds tens of pc cm⁻³ at b ≳
  0.5 r200. The code's behavior here is the inverse problem (it caps at 0.1 R_vir
  for the inner blow-up) but the outer-truncation sensitivity is unhandled.

### B. Galaxy clusters along sightlines, and high-Dec catalog coverage
- **Cluster catalogs and their declination reach** (the binding constraint for this
  sample): WHL (Wen, Han & Liu 2012; update Wen & Han 2015) and redMaPPer (Rykoff et
  al. 2014) are **SDSS-based and do not cover Dec > +70** (SDSS imaging in the N
  Galactic Cap ends near +60–65°). SZ catalogs ACT DR5 (Hilton et al. 2021) and
  SPT-SZ (Bleem et al. 2015), and X-ray eROSITA eRASS1 (Bulbul et al. 2024) are
  equatorial/southern. **The only catalogs with genuine Dec > +70 coverage are the
  all-sky ones: Planck PSZ2 (SZ; Planck Collab. 2016) and MCXC / MCXC-II (X-ray
  RASS-based; Piffaretti et al. 2011; MCXC-II 2024)** — both shallow and
  mass-limited there, so poor completeness for group-mass systems.
- **FRBs through clusters give meaningful DM but negligible scattering.** DSA-110
  FRBs in Abell 2310/2311 contribute ICM DM of 16–568 pc cm⁻³ but show no
  significant scattering (Connor et al. 2023). FRB 20190520B pierces two foreground
  clusters (b = 0.39 and 0.77 r200) contributing 450–640 pc cm⁻³ total halo DM, and
  the authors explicitly argue these clusters do **not** cause the burst's large τ
  (Lee et al. 2023).
- **DM vs impact parameter is steep**: ~300 pc cm⁻³ at 0.39 r200 falling to ~110 pc
  cm⁻³ at 0.77 r200 (Lee et al. 2023). Meaningful cluster DM requires the sightline
  within ~1–2 r200 (≲ a few Mpc for the most massive clusters); a **5 Mpc impact
  radius mostly returns negligible-DM near-misses** (this last step is an
  extrapolation from the observed profiles, not a directly stated literature
  result).
- Cluster turbulent ICM can in principle enhance scattering (Zhu, Feng et al. 2018),
  but geometric leverage for a cosmologically-distant cluster screen drives
  broadening to ≪ ms — negligible vs FRB τ (Ocker et al. 2022, scattering horizons;
  secondary).

### C. CGM/halo gas → DM and scattering models
- **mNFW gas profile** ρ_b(r) ∝ 1/[y^(1−α)(y0+y)^(2+α)], fiducial y0 = α = 2, f_b,halo
  = 0.75 (Prochaska & Zheng 2019) — **exactly the form the code implements and
  cites** (`scattering_predict.py:27-123`). FLIMFLAM uses the same form.
- **Macquart relation** ⟨DM_cosmic⟩ ∝ ∫ f_IGM(1+z)/E(z) dz (Macquart et al. 2020,
  Nature 581) — the baseline the code uses for cosmic DM
  (`sightline_budget.py:124-143`).
- **Geometric leverage**: τ ≈ 48.03 μs · A_τ F̃ DM_ℓ² G_scatt /[(1+z_ℓ)³ ν⁴], with
  G_scatt = 2 d_sl d_lo /(L d_so) (Ocker, Cordes & Chatterjee 2021). This is the
  canonical FRB-halo leverage paper; the code cites Macquart & Koay 2013 for the
  equivalent geometry (`scattering_predict.py:192-217`) and could additionally cite
  Ocker et al. 2021.
- **Cool-CGM cloudlet scattering**: foundational model is Vedantham & Phinney 2019
  on McCourt et al. 2018 "shattered" sub-pc cloudlets; refractive cloudlet lensing
  (Pradeep et al. 2023 — see citation caveat in *Synthesis*). But **CGM cloud-size
  limits** suggest realistic low-z cool CGM (n_e ≲ 10⁻² cm⁻³) adds little scattering
  unless clouds are ≲ 0.1 pc (Sammons et al. 2025 / cloud-size limits), and hot-CGM
  scattering is ≪ 1 μs at 1 GHz (Ocker et al. 2025). The code's
  `COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` (range 3–30) is therefore an **optimistic** cool-phase
  prior (`scattering_predict.py:254-262`).

### D. MgII absorbers as intervening cool-gas tracers
- **No published work ties an individual MgII absorber to a measured FRB DM or τ
  excess on the same sightline** — this is a genuine gap (a claimed link from a
  secondary source produced only hallucinated IDs and was discarded). MgII↔cool-CGM
  baseline relations are quasar-based: W(MgII 2796) declines with impact parameter,
  high covering fraction within ~100–150 kpc of L* galaxies (MAGIICAT — Nielsen et
  al. 2013; Lan & Mo 2018) — the relations the code's `predict_mgii_wr` and
  `cool_covering_fraction` encode (`scattering_predict.py:321-384`). MgII in
  groups/clusters: Anand et al. 2022. The DSA multi-halo burst uses COS-Halos
  N_HII(b,M⋆) — the same cool photoionized phase MgII traces — as its DM_igh
  template (Connor et al. 2024).

### E. Disconfirming evidence & limitations
- **Scattering is usually host/Milky-Way-dominated, not foreground-dominated.**
  Halos contribute to DM "but evidently not to scattering, which is dominated by
  host galaxies" (Cordes et al. 2022); measured intervening-halo τ is negligible for
  FRB 181112/191108 (Ocker et al. 2021); FRB 20190520B is explicitly host-dominated
  (Ocker et al. 2022, host-dominated); CHIME population modeling concurs (Chawla et
  al. 2025 / CHIME population). **Counterpoint:** the DSA multi-halo burst FRB
  20221219A is *not* host-dominated — an overcrowded intervening sightline can
  dominate (Connor et al. 2024). That is precisely the regime the co-detection
  sample is built to probe.
- **High-Dec photo-z incompleteness / catalog depth.** DESI Legacy photo-z degrade
  where spec-z training is sparse — directly the Dec > +70 case (Duncan 2022; Zhou
  et al. 2021; SOM photo-z analyses). A Rubin-era forecast finds the dominant error
  is **missing dim/high-z hosts** (catalog depth), degrading inferences ~47–62%, far
  more than photo-z scatter (~7%) (Rubin-era FRB photo-z forecast). SDSS
  spectroscopy largely excludes Dec > +70; GLADE+ is shallow; PS1 lacks
  spectroscopy — so the project's high-Dec search is photometry-limited and
  spec-z-starved. No paper quantifies FRB-foreground completeness specifically at
  Dec > +70 (gap).
- **False-association risk.** PATH quantifies chance coincidence (Aggarwal et al.
  2021); host association degrades once σ_loc ≳ 2″ (localization-precision bias
  study), so large-impact foreground association under coarse localization or
  photo-z-only redshifts is fragile. Radio selection (high-DM/low-scattering) skews
  the sample and biases foreground-incidence statistics (Seebeck, Shannon et al.
  2021). No FRB-specific paper gives a contamination number for intervening (vs
  host) association under photo-z only (gap).

## Synthesis

**The code implements a defensible, literature-grounded version of the standard
method, but a deliberately simplified one.** Its impact-parameter + (photo-z or
spec-z) cone search, mNFW halo DM, Macquart cosmic baseline, two-phase cool/hot
screen, and geometric leverage all map directly onto the canonical references
(Prochaska & Zheng 2019, Macquart et al. 2020, Ocker et al. 2021, FLIMFLAM), and
the modules cite them inline. What it does **not** do — and what FLIMFLAM does — is
reconstruct the Mpc-scale density field or jointly fit the baryon partition; the
code instead attributes per-halo columns deterministically and brackets the
nuisance astrophysics with a prior-predictive sensitivity layer. That is an
appropriate scope choice for a 12-sightline study, not a defect.

**The external consensus validates the code's central scientific verdict.** Across
Cordes et al. 2022, Ocker et al. 2021/2022, and CHIME population work, intervening
halos add DM but rarely scattering — exactly what the budget table reports
(predicted τ_interv negligible everywhere except Phineas). The one regime where this
flips — overcrowded sightlines (Connor et al. 2024) — is the regime this sample
targets, so the scientific payoff lives in the tail, and Phineas (3 foreground
galaxies, GALAXY_INTERIOR, τ_interv = 1.4 ms) is the natural first case to
scrutinize.

**The cluster search is the clearest weakness, and prior art explains why.**
Clusters are found only by NED object-type strings with a 5 Mpc impact, and **zero
clusters turn up** on any sightline. Two independent reasons: (1) at Dec +70°–74°,
the SDSS-based cluster catalogs (WHL, redMaPPer) have no coverage, and even the
notebook WHL cross-match cannot help there — only all-sky Planck PSZ2 and
MCXC/MCXC-II reach the band, both shallow; (2) a 5 Mpc impact mostly yields
negligible-DM near-misses, because meaningful cluster DM needs the sightline within
~1–2 r200, and clusters contribute essentially no scattering at any impact (Connor
et al. 2023, Lee et al. 2023). So the current cluster machinery is both
under-powered (wrong catalogs for this sky) and aimed at a low-yield regime.

### Gaps and open questions
- **Cluster catalog coverage at Dec > +70.** If clusters matter, the production
  search needs an all-sky catalog engine (Planck PSZ2 and/or MCXC-II), not NED-type
  strings — and the 5 Mpc radius should be re-examined against an r200-relative
  criterion. *(Light recommendation; design deferred to planning.)*
- **`COOL_CLUMP_BOOST = 10` is optimistic.** Recent cloud-size/turbulence limits
  (Ocker et al. 2025; cloud-size-limits study) push the realistic cool-CGM
  scattering contribution down; the lo bound (3) may be closer to reality. Worth a
  sensitivity knob already partly present in `sightline_sensitivity.py`.
- **MgII → FRB DM/τ is an unfilled gap in the literature.** The code already
  predicts MgII W_r and uses it to boost cool DM, but that proxy has **no direct
  FRB observational validation** anywhere — it is a modeling assumption, and a
  potential novelty angle if the sample can test it. Flag clearly in any write-up.
- **Outer-truncation (2·r200) DM sensitivity** is unhandled; Prochaska & Zheng warn
  it adds tens of pc cm⁻³ at moderate impact.
- **Citation to verify before publication.** `docs/codetection-science-plan.md:64`
  cites "Sammons 2309.07256" for CGM cloudlet lensing; the prior-art pass reports
  arXiv:2309.07256 is the cloudlet refractive-lensing paper but that its lead author
  is **not** Sammons (a separate Sammons et al. FRB-scattering paper may exist).
  Re-check the author/ID pairing via an ADS author search before this citation
  reaches a manuscript. *(Left in place pending confirmation; not silently edited
  because the correct attribution was not established this pass.)*

## References / Sources

### Code (commit `8ca87f7`)
- `galaxies/v2_0/config.py:9-17,21-56` — targets, catalogs, impact/redshift defaults
- `galaxies/v2_0/search.py:31-43,56-74,87-201,204-332` — cluster regex, foreground
  mask, dedup, PS1 backfill, search driver
- `galaxies/v2_0/engines.py:23-148` — NED / Vizier / PS1 engines
- `galaxies/v2_0/engines_extra.py` — opt-in DESI DR1 spec-z engine
- `galaxies/v2_0/enrichers.py` — opt-in cross-survey enrichment
- `galaxies/v2_0/build_unified.py:26-33,118-155,246-474` — mass ladder, per-galaxy
  CGM record assembly
- `galaxies/v2_0/scattering_predict.py:27-385` — mNFW DM, cool DM, MgII W_r,
  covering fraction, two-phase τ, g_scatt
- `galaxies/v2_0/sightline_budget.py:71-143,340-608` — DM/τ budget closure, caps,
  verdicts
- `galaxies/v2_0/sightline_sensitivity.py` — prior-predictive sensitivity layer
- `galaxies/wise-ps1-strm/wiseps1_beam_search.py`,
  `galaxies/tarrio_ps1_photz/find_impact_matches.py`,
  `galaxies/dr8_dec70_75/stack.py` — non-production / earlier search methods
- `notebooks/codetections/interveners.ipynb`, `galaxies/v2_0/analysis.ipynb` —
  exploratory WHL cluster cross-match (notebook-only)
- `results/search_summary.csv`, `results/sightline_dm_scattering_budget.md`,
  `results/*_galaxies.csv` — committed per-sightline outputs

### External (arXiv IDs; `[unverified]` = sub-agent-reported but not re-confirmed by the local hook offline this session)
- Prochaska & Zheng 2019, mNFW halo gas/DM — arXiv:1901.11051
- Macquart et al. 2020, Nature 581, Macquart relation — arXiv:2005.13161
- Khrykin, Lee, Simha et al. 2024, FLIMFLAM DR1 — arXiv:2402.00505
- Khrykin et al. 2023, FLIMFLAM excess-DM — arXiv:2303.07387
- Simha et al. 2020 (FRB 190608) — arXiv:2005.13157; Simha et al. 2021 (FRB 180924) — arXiv:2108.09881
- Ocker, Cordes & Chatterjee 2021, halo scattering + G_scatt — arXiv:2101.04784 [unverified]
- Cordes et al. 2022, DM/τ, host-dominated scattering — arXiv:2108.01172 [unverified]; scattering formalism Cordes et al. 2016 — arXiv:1605.05890 [unverified]
- Vedantham & Phinney 2019, CGM cool-clump scattering — arXiv:1811.10876 [unverified]
- Cloudlet refractive lensing — arXiv:2309.07256 [unverified] (author/ID pairing to re-verify; see Synthesis)
- Ocker et al. 2025, CGM turbulence microphysics — arXiv:2503.02329 [unverified]
- CGM cloud-size limits — arXiv:2504.19562 [unverified]
- Ocker et al. 2022, radio scattering horizons — arXiv:2203.16716
- Connor et al. 2023, DSA-110 FRBs in clusters (ICM DM + RM) — arXiv:2302.14788
- Lee et al. 2023, FRB 20190520B through 2 foreground clusters — arXiv:2306.05403
- Ocker et al. 2022, FRB 20190520B host-dominated — arXiv:2202.13458 [unverified]
- Connor et al. 2024, DSA-110 multi-halo FRB 20221219A — arXiv:2405.14182
- Law, Sharma et al. 2023, DSA-110 first FRB+host catalog — arXiv:2307.03344
- Aggarwal et al. 2021, PATH — arXiv:2102.10627; chance coincidence Bhandari et al. 2022 — arXiv:2009.10747; localization-precision bias — arXiv:2502.16791 [unverified]
- Cluster catalogs: WHL Wen/Han/Liu 2012 — arXiv:1202.6424 (update 2015 — arXiv:1503.04332); redMaPPer Rykoff et al. 2014 — arXiv:1303.3562; ACT DR5 Hilton et al. 2021 — arXiv:2009.11043; SPT-SZ Bleem et al. 2015 — arXiv:1409.0850; Planck PSZ2 2016 — arXiv:1502.01598; eROSITA eRASS1 cosmology companion — arXiv:2402.08458; MCXC Piffaretti et al. 2011 — arXiv:1007.1916, MCXC-II — arXiv:2402.01538
